evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: maintaining data quality, security, integrity and governance by effectively following regulatory requirements; end-to-end ownership of data quality; service level agreements met by implementing tests and processes
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Classified as rigour because responsibilities are dominated by 'regulatory requirements', 'data quality, security, integrity and governance', 'observability', and 'SLAs' — with no velocity or speed-to-ship signals.
evidence.domain_risk: Booking.com public-facing platform at scale; regulatory requirements explicitly named in responsibilities
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Classified as high because Booking.com is a public-facing product at massive scale with 'regulatory requirements' explicitly named as a governance obligation, and financial transactions (travel bookings) at scale.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Platform Team; Data Enablement Teams; Flights Data Enablement Team; ambitious modernisation programme
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Classified as mature because the organisation has multiple named data sub-teams with distinct charters (Platform, Enablement, Flights) and is running an enterprise-scale modernisation programme.
evidence.jd_authorship: Use standardised tooling and procedures to model and implement data pipelines which are performant, scalable, reliable, secure, well governed with required observability; end-to-end ownership of data quality
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Classified as mixed because technically precise bullets (observability, Data Vault/Kimball methodology, Kafka streaming) sit alongside generic boilerplate ('engage with other teams as needed to achieve business objectives').
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: self-serve analytics for many internal customers across departments; Providing a platform as a service; analysts and product teams
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Classified as internal_data because the primary output is the data platform itself — 'providing a platform as a service' and enabling 'self-serve analytics for internal customers across departments'.
evidence.autonomy_level: end-to-end ownership of data quality in our core datasets; adhere to the defined principles for architecture, quality and non-functional requirements
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Classified as mixed because 'end-to-end ownership' signals strategic accountability while 'adhere to defined principles for architecture' signals execution within a pre-defined framework.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Machine learning is mentioned as a platform team responsibility, but no AI tool usage or AI infrastructure enablement is expected of the candidate in their role.
evidence.testing_framing: end-to-end ownership of data quality in our core datasets and data pipelines; ensuring that service level agreements are met by implementing tests and processes
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Classified as responsibility because 'end-to-end ownership of data quality' and 'implementing tests and processes' to meet SLAs frame testing as a primary named accountability of the candidate.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: maintaining data quality, security, integrity and governance by effectively following regulatory requirements
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Classified as high because 'regulatory requirements', 'data quality, security, integrity and governance' appear in the first responsibility block as primary framing, indicating trust and compliance are central concerns.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: replacements for the current ad-hoc data solutions; ambitious modernisation programme focusing on infrastructure transition
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Classified as mixed because the role involves both replacing ad-hoc solutions with modern architecture (fix_scale) and building new Flights data products on the BDX platform (greenfield).
evidence.loss_aversion: maintaining data quality, security, integrity and governance by effectively following regulatory requirements — regulatory compliance and data trustworthiness are primary framings dominating the responsibilities section.
